prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "站立勾腿" (standing hamstring curl) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a standing hamstring curl: right leg supporting with foot flat on the ground and knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left leg lifted behind the body with knee bent so that the left foot heel is brought toward the left buttock (knee fully flexed), the left thigh remains roughly vertical (perpendicular to the floor); torso upright with neutral spine, right hand placed on a wall in front for balance; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hamstring group of the curling (left) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus of the curling leg and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group of the curling leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
